rows.notes: Clear heading. The three right-hand columns align well; the top-right numbers read unambiguously as 618 | 1 | 2.
Description legible. Amount columns well aligned; numbers read as 19 | 2 | 6 with clear digit shapes.
Description mostly legible ('Merton Rectory money 1/2 yr'). Amount columns align; reads as 14 | 7 | 3 though the final digit is slightly faint.
Short description 'Corn' on the line below Merton Rectory; amount reads clearly as 37 | 13 | 4 and columns align with other rows.
Text 'vicars Stipend' is abbreviated and slightly cramped. Amount columns align but the '1' in pounds is small; chosen reading 1 | 8 | 10 based on column position.
Handwriting for place-name 'Montacute' legible. The amount on the same horizontal band reads 1 | 8 | 10 but this line is crowded with neighbouring small figures, so modest confidence.
Place name 'Newington J.' is legible. The amount is a larger figure (181 | 7 | 5) and aligns with the pounds/shillings/pence columns clearly.
A small line with the single word 'Corn' appears beneath Newington J. but no distinct amount is visible on that exact line (likely a sub-item whose amount is on a sibling line).
Description is somewhat condensed but readable as 'Pethram Qualification viz: Godalming 24'. Amount reads as 4 | 10 | (no pence). The pence column is blank for this line.
Multiple sub-items in the description are present; the amount reads clearly as 8 | 13 | 9½ (pence shows a half symbol). Alignment with column lines is clear.
Place name is ambiguous (handwriting unclear); chosen 'Withersyn/Wotkensyn' based on letter shapes. Amount columns read 14 | 9 | 4 but the description is moderately uncertain.
Description 'Yarnton Rectory' and 'Tenements' readable. Amount reads clearly as 46 | 17 | 10 and columns align well.
Short description 'Revd. Land Tax' clear. Amount 13 | 5 | 9 is distinct and validated against column positions.
The line appears as a place-name 'Waldron' followed by dashes (no amount on that line). The dashes suggest a break or section divider rather than a numeric entry.
This is the subtotal line for the above block. The large total '963 | 17 | 2½' is well aligned and the halfpence symbol is evident.
Clear centered header (Latin) marking the start of the receipts for the current year. No amounts on this header line.
Description and amount '10 | 10 | 0' are clearly written and align with the columns.
Description 'Bushey Tenements' readable. Amount reads well as 12 | 17 | 2.
Line reads 'Kidlington - Cottages' with amount clearly 4 | 9 | 5; columns unambiguous.
Description 'Vicarage - money' readable. Amount shows 3 | 15 | (no pence written). Pence assumed zero; the pence cell for this line is faint/blank.
Second occurrence of 'Revd. Land Tax' in this section. Amount reads clearly 4 | 12 | 9.
Description legible as 'Little Tew timber - money 1/2 yr'. Amount registers as 4 | 13 | (blank pence). Pence assumed zero; alignment supports this.
Place name reads 'Roy Wittenham' (handwriting spellings approximate). Amount appears as 4 | 4 | 0; the pence column is blank.

Only a single '6' appears on this line in the mid-right column. It could be either 6 shillings or 6 pence; based on its horizontal alignment (closer to the shillings column) it is recorded as 0 | 6 | 0. Handwriting is small so confidence is low.
Sub-heading 'Merton Rectory (Corn) 1/2 yr' readable. Amount 15 | 3 | 4 is distinct in the columns.
Follow-up line 'money' for Merton Rectory; amount 37 | 13 | 4 matches the same pattern seen earlier for Merton entries and is clearly written.

Handwritten label 'Vicars Stipend / yr' appears and a number sits to the right; there is some ambiguity because a similar 37 | 13 | 4 appears nearby. I have recorded it here but the precise association of the figure to this sublabel is moderately uncertain.
Line 'Shortridge Trustees money' (spelling as on page) is clear and the amount 210 | 7 | 9 is large and legible with excellent alignment.
Place 'Hart Hall' visible; the numbers to the right read 1 | 13 | 4 though they are somewhat small—interpretation based on column alignment.
A small single '1' sits on this line in the pounds column (shillings/pence blank). The place name 'Waldron' again appears and the single pound reading is chosen from column position.

rows.description: Gardener 1/2 yr to Lady Day 1855 P.T.D. for y
rows.notes: Detailed note about gardener and period to Lady Day 1855 is legible though cramped. Amount reads 14 | 1 | 8; all three digits are visible.
This line is a continuation/note relating to the gardener line above. No separate amount is present on this exact line.
Final total at bottom-right reads approximately '375 | 15 | 7' but the last digits are somewhat worn/faded; columns align but the last digit's ink is faint, so confidence is moderate.
